SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.hazid

Class HazopConsequenceAutoPopulator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.hazid.HazopConsequenceAutoPopulator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class HazopConsequenceAutoPopulator extends Object implements Serializable
Auto-populates HAZOP worksheet consequence and safeguard columns by mapping each guide-word/parameter deviation to the NeqSim calculation that quantifies the scenario.

A first-pass HAZOP grid generated by HAZOPTemplate.generateGrid(Parameter...) or HAZOPTemplate.fromProcessSystem(neqsim.process.processmodel.ProcessSystem) contains only "TBD" placeholders. This populator replaces those placeholders with simulation-backed consequence mechanisms, the recommended NeqSim calculator, the governing standard, and a typical safeguard, drawn from the static catalogue in catalogue().

The catalogue encodes the HAZOP deviation → calculation map:

HAZOP deviation to NeqSim calculation mapping
Deviation NeqSim calculator Standard
MORE PRESSURE (overpressure) ReliefValveSizing / SettleOutPressureAnalyzer / GasBlowbyAnalyzer API 520/521
LESS PRESSURE (vacuum collapse) VacuumCollapseAnalyzer API 521 / ASME
LESS TEMPERATURE (autorefrigeration / brittle) MDMTCalculator / DepressurizationSimulator ASME UCS-66 / API 521
NO FLOW (deadhead) PumpDeadheadAnalyzer API 610
MORE FLOW (surge / erosion) getUtilizationSnapshot surge/velocity check API 14E / 617
REVERSE FLOW (backflow / surge) WaterHammerStudy API 521
AS_WELL_AS COMPOSITION (contamination) phase / hydrate / separator screening API 521
OTHER_THAN REACTION (runaway) RunawayReactionAnalyzer DIERS / API 521
MORE TEMPERATURE (fire case) TrappedLiquidFireRuptureStudy / PfpDemandCalculator API 521

References:

  • IEC 61882 — HAZOP studies application guide
  • API 521 — Pressure-relieving and Depressuring Systems
  • API 520 — Sizing, Selection, and Installation of Pressure-relieving Devices
Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • HazopConsequenceAutoPopulator

      public HazopConsequenceAutoPopulator()

  • Method Details

    • buildCatalogue

      private static Map<String,HazopConsequenceMapping> buildCatalogue()
      Build the static deviation → calculation catalogue.
      Returns:
      an insertion-ordered map keyed by key(GuideWord, Parameter)

    • add

      private static void add(Map<String,HazopConsequenceMapping> map, HazopConsequenceMapping mapping)
      Insert a mapping into the catalogue keyed by its guide-word and parameter.
      Parameters:
      map - the catalogue being built
      mapping - the mapping to insert

    • key

      private static String key(HAZOPTemplate.GuideWord guideWord, HAZOPTemplate.Parameter parameter)
      Build the catalogue key for a guide-word/parameter pair.
      Parameters:
      guideWord - the guide-word
      parameter - the parameter
      Returns:
      a stable string key

    • catalogue

      public List<HazopConsequenceMapping> catalogue()
      Get the immutable list of all consequence mappings in the catalogue.
      Returns:
      the catalogue mappings, in declaration order

    • mappingFor

      public HazopConsequenceMapping mappingFor(HAZOPTemplate.GuideWord guideWord, HAZOPTemplate.Parameter parameter)
      Look up the consequence mapping for a guide-word/parameter deviation.
      Parameters:
      guideWord - the guide-word (must not be null)
      parameter - the parameter (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the matching mapping, or null if the catalogue has no entry for the deviation

    • populate

      public HAZOPTemplate populate(HAZOPTemplate source)
      Produce a new HAZOP node with consequence and safeguard columns auto-populated from the catalogue.

      The source template is not modified (HAZOPTemplate.HAZOPDeviation rows are immutable). For each deviation, if a catalogue mapping exists, the placeholder consequence/safeguard/recommendation cells are filled with the mapped values; already-filled (non-"TBD") cells are preserved. Deviations without a catalogue entry are copied unchanged.

      Parameters:
      source - the HAZOP node to populate (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a new HAZOPTemplate with the same node id and design intent and populated deviation rows
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if source is null

    • fill

      private String fill(String existing, String mapped)
      Replace a placeholder cell with a mapped value, preserving any existing non-placeholder content.
      Parameters:
      existing - the existing cell content (may be null or "TBD")
      mapped - the mapped value to use when the cell is empty/placeholder
      Returns:
      the existing content if meaningful, otherwise the mapped value

    • fillRecommendation

      private String fillRecommendation(String existing, HazopConsequenceMapping mapping)
      Build the recommendation cell, citing the recommended NeqSim calculator and governing standard when the existing recommendation is empty.
      Parameters:
      existing - the existing recommendation (may be null or "TBD")
      mapping - the catalogue mapping for the deviation
      Returns:
      the existing recommendation if meaningful, otherwise a calculator/standard citation

    • catalogueToJson

      public String catalogueToJson()
      Serialise the full catalogue to a pretty-printed JSON array.
      Returns:
      JSON representation of all catalogue mappings

    • quantify

      public List<HazopConsequenceFinding> quantify(ProcessSystem process, HazopQuantificationLimits limits)
      Quantify the simulation-backed HAZOP consequences of a live flowsheet into deterministic PASS / EXCEEDS findings.

      Where populate(HAZOPTemplate) only states which calculator applies, this method actually evaluates the consequences that depend on the thermodynamic engine and that can be read directly from the post-run stream state, then compares each computed value against a design limit from limits:

      • Compressors and expanders — MORE TEMPERATURE: the discharge temperature (the result of polytropic compression plus a flash) is compared against the maximum allowable discharge temperature.
      • Valves — LESS TEMPERATURE: the outlet temperature (the result of an isenthalpic Joule-Thomson flash) is compared against the minimum design metal temperature, screening for low-temperature embrittlement.

      The flowsheet must already have been run (for example via process.run()) so the outlet streams carry solved conditions. Units with no thermodynamically quantifiable scenario produce no finding here; their catalogue text is still available through populate(HAZOPTemplate). Any read or calculation that fails degrades to a HazopConsequenceFinding.Verdict.NOT_EVALUATED finding rather than throwing, mirroring the tolerant flowsheet walk used elsewhere.

      Parameters:
      process - the run flowsheet to evaluate (must not be null)
      limits - the design limits to compare computed consequences against; defaults are used when null
      Returns:
      one finding per quantifiable deviation, in flowsheet order (possibly empty)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if process is null

    • evaluateDischargeTemperature

      private HazopConsequenceFinding evaluateDischargeTemperature(String nodeId, ProcessEquipmentInterface unit, String name, HazopQuantificationLimits limits)
      Evaluate the MORE TEMPERATURE consequence for a compressor or expander: discharge over-temperature.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the HAZOP node identifier
      unit - the compressor or expander unit
      name - the unit name
      limits - the resolved design limits
      Returns:
      a discharge-temperature finding, or a NOT_EVALUATED finding if the temperature cannot be read

    • evaluateAutoRefrigeration

      private HazopConsequenceFinding evaluateAutoRefrigeration(String nodeId, ProcessEquipmentInterface unit, String name, HazopQuantificationLimits limits)
      Evaluate the LESS TEMPERATURE consequence for a valve: Joule-Thomson auto-refrigeration below the MDMT.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the HAZOP node identifier
      unit - the valve unit
      name - the unit name
      limits - the resolved design limits
      Returns:
      an auto-refrigeration finding, or a NOT_EVALUATED finding if the temperature cannot be read

    • outletTemperatureCelsius

      private Double outletTemperatureCelsius(ProcessEquipmentInterface unit)
      Read the temperature of a unit's first outlet stream in degrees Celsius, tolerating equipment that throws.
      Parameters:
      unit - the unit operation
      Returns:
      the outlet temperature in degrees Celsius, or null if no outlet stream is available or the read fails
